overview.title: arc-evaluating v2 — preflight, analyzer, claim extraction, dashboard consolidation
overview.description: The v1 eval surface is statistically sound but physically scattered and operationally weak: preflight is prose-only, the v1 analyzer layers LLM judgment on top of already-quantified results, k=3 runs can produce SHIP without any confidence interval, graders cannot critique their own assertions or surface blind spots, and the live dashboard lacks the post-hoc review UX needed for iteration. v2 consolidates the eval surface into the skill folder (agents plus dashboard), tightens the verdict layer by introducing INSUFFICIENT_DATA, automates scenario validity through preflight/lint/audit CLIs, extends the grading pipeline with discovered-claim and weak-assertion channels, and merges real-time monitoring with skill-creator-style post-hoc review into a single dashboard. It also wires arc-evaluating into the arc-using Discipline Skills routing table so the shipping gate is actively triggered rather than remembered.

includes.feature: Folder consolidation: eval agents and dashboard move into skills/arc-evaluating/, with plugin.json registering nested agent paths so subagent_type dispatch still resolves
Grader pipeline extensions: per-trial duration_ms and token counts, discovered_claims[] and weak_assertions[] fields on grading.json, and the trigger rule for blind comparator supplementation
New CLI subcommands arc eval preflight, arc eval lint, arc eval audit — automating scenario validity, format checks, and cross-benchmark assertion auditing
Agent lifecycle changes: rename v1 comparator to eval-analyzer and strip its verdict authority; add eval-blind-comparator for paired-preference rating
Verdict tightening and routing integration: INSUFFICIENT_DATA when k<5, and an arc-using Discipline Skills routing entry for arc-evaluating
Dashboard consolidation and UI redesign: 2-tab layout, auto-saving feedback textboxes, arrow-key navigation, SSE real-time channel retained at the new skill-folder location

SKILL.md body discipline: triggering-only description, 1800-word Comprehensive-tier body, progressive disclosure into new references files, Rationalization Table and Red Flags updated for v2, TDD-for-docs applied to the edit, pytest validators kept green

excludes.reason: Full opt-in --blind mode where paired preference becomes the primary verdict. Only the minimal supplementary signal ships in v2; the paradigm shift is deferred until the supplementary signal demonstrates value.

Static HTML export via a Python-based generate_review.py pipeline. arcforge stays on its Node.js SSE-first stack; the dashboard will keep real-time updates rather than emitting portable static bundles.
Train/test split for skill-description trigger optimization. If that capability is needed it belongs in a dedicated skill such as arc-tuning-descriptions, not inside arc-evaluating.
Restructuring of the three-scope evaluation taxonomy (skill / agent / workflow). The scope framework is preserved as-is; v2 changes the machinery within each scope but not the scopes themselves.

Migration of scripts/lib/eval*.js into the skill folder. The canonical-engine rule in .claude/rules/architecture.md wins; canonical library code stays in scripts/lib/ and skill-owned tooling stays in the skill folder.
Automated mutation of existing scenario files under evals/scenarios/. Those remain untouched by v2 and will only change if authors choose to adopt the new CLI subcommands.
